center.name: 自然系统第十版
center.summary: 自然系统第十版是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联64种鸟类，包括南方鶴鴕、黑嘴樹鴨、加拿大雁等64种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

birds.evidence: | status = NT | status_system = IUCN3.1 | status_ref = | status2 = CITES_A2 | status2_system = CITES | status2_ref = | taxon = Ardeotis arabs | authority = (Linnaeus, 1758) | synonyms = Otis arabs }} thumb| Ardeotis arabs - MHNT The Arabian bustard (Ardeotis arabs) is a species of bustard which is found across the Sahel region of Africa and south western Arabia

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The black-capped lory (Lorius lory) also known as western black-capped lory or the tricolored lory, is a parrot found in New Guinea and adjacent smaller islands

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The black-headed canary (Serinus alario) is a species of finch found in Lesotho, Namibia and South Africa

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The common ground dove (Columbina passerina) is a small bird that inhabits the southern United States, parts of Central America, the Caribbean and northern South America

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The golden-headed manakin (Ceratopipra erythrocephala) is a small passerine bird which breeds in tropical Central and South America in both wet and dry forests, secondary growth and plantations

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The Guianan red cotinga (Phoenicircus carnifex) is a species of bird in the family Cotingidae, the cotingas. It is found in Brazil, French Guiana, Guyana, Suriname, and Venezuela.Remsen, J

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The Jamaican tody (Todus todus) is a species of bird in the genus Todus endemic to Jamaica. Local names for the Jamaican tody include rasta bird, robin and robin redbreast

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The crested myna (Acridotheres cristatellus), also known as the Chinese starling, is a species of starling in the genus Acridotheres native to southeastern China and Indochina.Feare, C., Craig, A., Croucher, B., Shields, C., Komolphalin, K

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The blue jay (Cyanocitta cristata) is a passerine bird in the family Corvidae, native to eastern North America

birds.evidence: alt=Spoon-billed sandpiper in breeding plumage|thumb|A "spoonie" on the breeding grounds in Chukotka, Siberia, June 2015. The spoon-billed sandpiper (Calidris pygmaea) is a small wader which breeds on the coasts of the Bering Sea and winters in Southeast Asia

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The southern cassowary (Casuarius casuarius), also known as double-wattled cassowary, Australian cassowary, or two-wattled cassowary, is a large, flightless, mostly black bird, found in Indonesia, Papua New Guinea, and northeastern Australia

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: | image2 = Charadrius vociferus.ogg | genus = Charadrius | species = vociferus | authority = Linnaeus, 1758 | range_map = Charadrius vociferus map.svg | range_map_caption = }} The killdeer (Charadrius vociferus) is a large plover found in the Americas

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The sun conure (Aratinga solstitialis), also known as the sun parakeet, is a medium-sized, vibrantly colored parrot native to northeastern South America

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The blue waxbill (Uraeginthus angolensis), also called southern blue waxbill, blue-breasted waxbill, southern cordon-bleu, blue-cheeked cordon-bleu, blue-breasted cordon-bleu and Angola cordon-bleu, is a common species of estrildid finch found in Southern Africa

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The northern mockingbird (Mimus polyglottos) is a mockingbird commonly found in North America, of the family Mimidae

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The carrion crow (Corvus corone) is a passerine bird of the family Corvidae, native to western Europe and the eastern Palearctic

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The black-rumped flameback (Dinopium benghalense), also known as the lesser golden-backed woodpecker or lesser goldenback, is a woodpecker found widely distributed in the Indian subcontinent. It is one of the few woodpeckers that are seen in urban areas

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: | author-link=Walter Joseph Bock | year=1994 | title=History and Nomenclature of Avian Family-Group Names | series=Bulletin of the American Museum of Natural History | volume= 222 | publisher=American Museum of Natural History | location=New York | pages=146, 245 | hdl=2246/830 | url=http://digitallibrary.amnh.org/handle/2246/830 }} | display_parents = 2 | taxon = Upupa | authority = Linnaeus, 1758 | type_species = Upupa epops (Eurasian hoopoe) | type_species_authority = Linnaeus, 1758 | subdivision_ranks = Species | subdivision = *†Upupa antaios * Upupa epops * Upupa marginata }} Hoopoes () are colourful birds found across Africa, Asia, and Europe, notable for their distinctive "crown" of feathers which can be raised or lowered at will

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The bearded reedling (Panurus biarmicus) is a small, long-tailed passerine bird found in reed beds near water in the temperate zone of Eurasia

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: nisoriaBali, Indonesia | status = LC | status_system = IUCN3.1 | status_ref = | genus = Lonchura | species = punctulata | authority = (Linnaeus, 1758) | range_map = Areale Lonchura punctulata.jpg | range_map_alt = Native range | range_map_caption = Native range | synonyms = *Loxia punctulata }} The scaly-breasted munia or spotted munia (Lonchura punctulata), known in the pet trade as nutmeg mannikin or spice finch, is a sparrow-sized estrildid finch native to tropical Asia

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The Indian pied myna (Gracupica contra) is a species of starling found in the Indian subcontinent

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The Eurasian treecreeper or common treecreeper (Certhia familiaris) is a small passerine bird also known in the British Isles, where it is the only living member of its genus, simply as treecreeper

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The common grackle (Quiscalus quiscula) is an icterid bird found in large numbers through much of North America

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: |year=2019 |amends=2018 |title=Larus canus |volume=2019 |article-number=e.T22694308A155576460 |doi=10.2305/IUCN.UK.2018-2.RLTS.T22694308A155576460.en |access-date=13 April 2021}} | genus = Larus | species = canus | authority = Linnaeus, 1758 | range_map = Larus canus map.svg }} The common gull (Larus canus) is a gull that breeds in cool temperate regions of the Palearctic from Iceland and Scotland east to Kamchatka in the Russian Far East

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: | image2 = Dark-eyed junco - Red-backed race (Junco hyemalis dorsalis) Las Cruces, New Mexico, USA.jpg | image2_caption = Red-backed race | genus = Junco | species = hyemalis | authority = (Linnaeus, 1758) | range_map = Junco hyemalis map.svg | range_map_caption = Range in North America | synonyms = }} The dark-eyed junco (Junco hyemalis) is a species of junco, a group of small, grayish New World sparrows

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The wood stork (Mycteria americana) is a large wading bird in the family Ciconiidae (storks)

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The king vulture (Sarcoramphus papa) is a large bird found in Central and South America

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The Indian silverbill or white-throated munia (Euodice malabarica) is a small passerine bird found in the Indian subcontinent and adjoining regions that was formerly considered to include the closely related African silverbill (Euodice cantans)

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The Eurasian spoonbill (Platalea leucorodia), or common spoonbill, is a wading bird of the ibis and spoonbill family Threskiornithidae, native to Europe, Africa and Asia

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The redwing (Turdus iliacus) is a bird in the thrush family, Turdidae, native to Europe and the Palearctic, slightly smaller than the related song thrush

birds.evidence: fusca (Sri Lanka) thumb|Call | status = LC | status_system = IUCN3.1 | status_ref = | genus = Halcyon | species = smyrnensis | authority = (Linnaeus, 1758) | range_map = White-throated Kingfisher Range.JPG | range_map_caption = (includes gularis) | synonyms = Alcedo smyrnensis }} The white-throated kingfisher (Halcyon smyrnensis) also known as the white-breasted kingfisher is a tree kingfisher, widely distributed in Asia from the Sinai east through the Indian subcontinent to China and Indonesia

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The belted kingfisher (Megaceryle alcyon) is a large, conspicuous water kingfisher, native to North America

birds.evidence: |year=2024 |title=Amadina erythrocephala |volume=2024 |article-number=e.T22719932A263790372 |doi=10.2305/IUCN.UK.2024-2.RLTS.T22719932A263790372.en |access-date=22 February 2025}} | genus = Amadina | species = erythrocephala | authority = (Linnaeus, 1758) }} The red-headed finch (Amadina erythrocephala) (also known as the paradise finch) is a common species of estrildid finch found in Africa

birds.evidence: cyanopterusCopan, Honduras | status = LC | status_system = IUCN3.1 | status_ref = | status2 = CITES_A1 | status2_system = CITES | status2_ref = | genus = Ara | species = macao | authority = (Linnaeus, 1758) | range_map = Distribution Ara macao.svg | range_map_caption = | synonyms = Psittacus macao }} thumb|Copan, Honduras thumb|A partnered pair of wild scarlet macaws rub against each other in Costa Rica The scarlet macaw (Ara macao) also called the red-and-yellow macaw, red-and-blue macaw or red-breasted macaw, is a large yellow, red and blue Neotropical parrot native to humid evergreen forests of the Americas

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: thumb|Adult males and females are identical in size and plumage. The red-headed woodpecker (Melanerpes erythrocephalus) is a mid-sized woodpecker found in temperate North America

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The red-breasted merganser (Mergus serrator) is a duck species that is native to much of the temperate to subarctic Northern Hemisphere

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The red knot or just knot (Calidris canutus) is a medium-sized shorebird which breeds in tundra and the Arctic Cordillera in the far north of Canada, Europe, and Russia

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: britannica recorded in Gloucestershire, England | taxon = Carduelis carduelis | authority = (Linnaeus, 1758) | range_map = CarduelisCarduelisIUCN.svg | range_map_caption = | synonyms = Fringilla carduelis }} The European goldfinch or simply referred to as the goldfinch (Carduelis carduelis) is a small passerine bird in the finch family that is native to the Palearctic zone in Europe, northern Africa, and western Asia

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The red-breasted parakeet (Psittacula alexandri) is a parrot native to Southeast Asia

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The emerald dove or common emerald dove (Chalcophaps indica), also called Asian emerald dove and grey-capped emerald dove, is a widespread resident breeding pigeon native to the tropical and subtropical parts of the Indian subcontinent and Southeast Asia

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The American flamingo (Phoenicopterus ruber) is a large species of flamingo native to the West Indies, northern South America (including the Galápagos Islands) and the Yucatán Peninsula

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The Indian peafowl (Pavo cristatus), also known as the common peafowl, or blue peafowl, is a species of peafowl native to the Indian subcontinent

birds.evidence: Geoffroy Saint-Hilaire]], 1809 | species = foetidus | authority = (Linnaeus, 1758) | synonyms = * Gracula foetida (protonym) | range_map = Gymnoderus foetidus map.svg }} The bare-necked fruitcrow (Gymnoderus foetidus) is a species of bird in the family Cotingidae, the cotingas. It is found in every mainland South American country except Argentina, Chile, Uruguay, and Paraguay.Remsen, J

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: thumb|Breeding male, Norway The long-tailed duck (Clangula hyemalis) is a medium-sized sea duck that breeds in the tundra and taiga regions of the arctic and winters along the northern coastlines of the Atlantic and Pacific Oceans

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The northern bald ibis, hermit ibis, or Waldrapp (Geronticus eremita) is a migratory Old World ibis found in open areas such as grasslands, rocky mountains, and semi-deserts, often close to running water

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: |year=2021 |title=Bubo scandiacus |volume=2021 |article-number=e.T22689055A205475036 |doi=10.2305/IUCN.UK.2021-3.RLTS.T22689055A205475036.en |access-date=3 July 2024}} | status2 = CITES_A2 | status2_system = CITES | status2_ref = | genus = Bubo | species = scandiacus | authority = (Linnaeus, 1758) | synonyms = *Strix scandiaca *Strix nyctea *Nyctea scandiaca (Linnaeus, 1758) | range_map = Cypron-Range Bubo scandiacus.svg | range_map_caption = }} The snowy owl (Bubo scandiacus), also known as the polar owl, the white owl and the Arctic owl, is a large, white owl of the true owl family. Snowy owls are native to the Arctic regions of both North America and the Palearctic, breeding mostly on the tundra. It has a number of unique adaptations to its habitat and lifestyle, which are quite distinct from other extant owls.{{cite book | last =Voous | first = Karel H
